source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure AWS Config is enabled in all regions

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure AWS Config is enabled in all regions
input_row.description: AWS Config is a web service that performs configuration management of supported AWS resources within your account and delivers log files to you. The recorded information includes the configuration items (AWS resources), relationships between configuration items (AWS resources), and any configuration changes between resources. It is recommended that AWS Config be enabled in all regions.
input_row.rationale: The AWS configuration item history captured by AWS Config enables security analysis, resource change tracking, and compliance auditing. Impact: Enabling AWS Config in all regions provides comprehensive visibility into resource configurations, enhancing security and compliance monitoring. However, this may incur additional costs and require proper configuration management.
input_row.audit: Process to evaluate AWS Config configuration per region: From Console: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the AWS Config console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/config/. 2. On the top right of the console select the target region. 3. If a Config Recorder is enabled in this region, you should navigate to the Settings page from the navigation menu on the left-hand side. If a Config Recorder is not yet enabled in this region, proceed to the remediation steps. 4. Ensure "Record all resources supported in this region" is checked. 5. Ensure "Include global resources (e.g., AWS IAM resources)" is checked, unless it is enabled in another region (this is only required in one region). 6. Ensure the correct S3 bucket has been defined. 7. Ensure the correct SNS topic has been defined. 8. Repeat steps 2 to 7 for each region. From Command Line: 1. Run this command to show all AWS Config Recorders and their properties: aws configservice describe-configuration-recorders 2. Evaluate the output to ensure that all recorders have a recordingGroup object which includes "allSupported": true. Additionally, ensure that at least one recorder has "includeGlobalResourceTypes": true. Note: There is one more parameter, "ResourceTypes," in the recordingGroup object. We don't need to check it, as whenever we set "allSupported" to true, AWS enforces the resource types to be empty ("ResourceTypes": []). Sample output: { "ConfigurationRecorders": [ { "recordingGroup": { "allSupported": true, "resourceTypes": [], "includeGlobalResourceTypes": true }, "roleARN": "arn:aws:iam::<AWS_Account_ID>:role/service- role/<config-role-name>", "name": "default" } ] } 3. Run this command to show the status for all AWS Config Recorders: aws configservice describe-configuration-recorder-status 4. In the output, find recorders with name key matching the recorders that were evaluated in step 2. Ensure that they include "recording": true and "lastStatus": "SUCCESS".
input_row.remediation: To implement AWS Config configuration: From Console: 1. Select the region you want to focus on in the top right of the console. 2. Click Services. 3. Click Config. 4. If a Config Recorder is enabled in this region, navigate to the Settings page from the navigation menu on the left-hand side. If a Config Recorder is not yet enabled in this region, select "Get Started". 5. Select "Record all resources supported in this region". 6. Choose to include global resources (IAM resources). 7. Specify an S3 bucket in the same account or in another managed AWS account. 8. Create an SNS Topic from the same AWS account or another managed AWS account. From Command Line: 1. Ensure there is an appropriate S3 bucket, SNS topic, and IAM role per the AWS Config Service prerequisites. 2. Run this command to create a new configuration recorder: aws configservice put-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder name=<config-recorder-name>,roleARN=arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:role/<iam-role> --recording-group allSupported=true,includeGlobalResourceTypes=true 3. Create a delivery channel configuration file locally which specifies the channel attributes, populated from the prerequisites set up previously: { "name": "<delivery-channel-name>", "s3BucketName": "<bucket-name>", "snsTopicARN": "arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account-id>:<sns-topic>", "configSnapshotDeliveryProperties": { "deliveryFrequency": "Twelve_Hours" } } 4. Run this command to create a new delivery channel, referencing the json configuration file made in the previous step: aws configservice put-delivery-channel --delivery-channel file://<delivery- channel-file>.json 5. Start the configuration recorder by running the following command: aws configservice start-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder-name <config-recorder-name> References: 1. CCE-78917-2 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/configservi ce/describe-configuration-recorder-status.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/configservi ce/describe-configuration-recorders.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/gs-cli-prereq.html
input_row.impact: Enabling AWS Config in all regions provides comprehensive visibility into resource configurations, enhancing security and compliance monitoring. However, this may incur additional costs and require proper configuration management.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The audit control requires checking the status and configuration of AWS Config across all regions, which can be fully verified using AWS APIs, boto3 SDK, or AWS CLI commands. The details provided in the "audit" section outline specific AWS CLI commands that make it clear this verification process can be automated. 

- The `describe-configuration-recorders` and `describe-configuration-recorder-status` AWS CLI commands and their equivalent boto3 SDK methods allow fetching detailed information about configuration recorders across all regions.
- The CLI commands and API calls listed in the audit process provide comprehensive details regarding the recording group's properties, such as "allSupported" and "includeGlobalResourceTypes," and confirm whether recordings are active and successful.
- Since AWS Config is a standard service with well-documented APIs, no manual intervention or visual checks are needed; the necessary checks can be automated using boto3 or AWS CLI.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_config_service_all_regions_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

The automated program will accomplish the following:

1. **Enumerate All Regions**:
   - Use the AWS SDK or CLI to obtain a list of all available AWS regions (`ec2.describe_regions()` for boto3 or `aws ec2 describe-regions` for CLI).

2. **Check AWS Config in Each Region**:
   - For each region, use `boto3` or equivalent AWS CLI commands to:
     - Call `configservice.describe_configuration_recorders()` to verify the presence of configuration recorders.
     - Validate that the configuration recorders have the `recordingGroup` object with `"allSupported": true`.
     - Ensure that at least one recorder has `"includeGlobalResourceTypes": true`.

3. **Check Recording Status**:
   - Use `configservice.describe_configuration_recorder_status()` to ensure each recorder is actively recording, indicated by `"recording": true` and `"lastStatus": "SUCCESS"`.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - **Pass**: All configuration recorders exist in each region, with `allSupported` set to true, one recorder including global resource types, and all recorders actively recording successfully.
   - **Fail**: If any region lacks a configuration recorder or has any recorder not configured properly, or if the recorders are not active or successful.

This approach guarantees comprehensive checks using AWS's existing infrastructure and APIs, adhering to cloud security posture management best practices and AWS CIS guidelines.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The status and configuration of AWS Config can be effectively checked using AWS APIs, boto3 SDK, or AWS CLI commands. The tasks outlined can be automated without the need for manual intervention, leveraging AWS's robust API capabilities.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- The automation details are technically accurate. The use of `describe-configuration-recorders` and `describe-configuration-recorder-status` via AWS CLI and their boto3 equivalents (`configservice.describe_configuration_recorders()` and `configservice.describe_configuration_recorder_status()`) are valid and appropriate for the task.
- The steps for checking the presence and configuration of AWS Config recorders, as well as their recording status, are correctly outlined.
- The program name `aws_config_service_all_regions_enabled` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, though it could be more specific about what is being checked (e.g., `aws_config_service_recorders_status_all_regions`).

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_config_service_recorders_status_all_regions` to more clearly indicate that the status of configuration recorders across all regions is being checked.
- **Additional Considerations:** Ensure that the script handles potential exceptions, such as regions where AWS Config is not supported or API call failures due to permissions issues.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS CloudFormation StackSets to ensure AWS Config is enabled across all regions as a preventive measure, although this is more of a configuration management task than an audit check.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of AWS APIs and CLI commands is appropriate for automating this compliance check. The technical details are sound, and the approach aligns with AWS security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Update the program name to `aws_config_service_recorders_status_all_regions` for improved clarity and specificity.
- The assessment is otherwise accurate and should be kept as-is, with the suggested modification to the program name for better clarity.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS Config status and configuration can be fully audited using AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). All necessary properties—such as whether a configuration recorder is enabled, its status, and whether it is recording all supported resources—are exposed via the AWS Config API. No manual console inspection or non-API-accessible information is required. The process is entirely automatable and repeatable across all regions.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_config_service_recorder_all_regions_enabled_and_recording_all_resources

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: AWS Config (Configuration Recorders) across all AWS regions
- API/CLI Method: 
  - List regions: `boto3.client('ec2').describe_regions()`
  - For each region:
    - `boto3.client('config').describe_configuration_recorders()`
    - `boto3.client('config').describe_configuration_recorder_status()`
    - `boto3.client('config').describe_delivery_channels()`
- Properties to Check:
  - At least one configuration recorder exists in each region
  - The configuration recorder is enabled and status is `recording=True`
  - The recorder's `recordingGroup` has `allSupported=True` and `includeGlobalResourceTypes=True`
  - A delivery channel is configured and active
- Pass Criteria:
  - In every AWS region:
    - AWS Config recorder exists and is enabled (`recording=True`)
    - Recorder is set to record all supported resources and global resource types
    - Delivery channel is configured and not in error
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any region where:
    - No configuration recorder exists
    - Recorder is not enabled/recording
    - Not all resources or global resources are being recorded
    - Delivery channel is missing or in error

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The second opinion suggested a slightly more descriptive program name, which has been adopted for clarity and specificity.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
